findings: Multiple feature pairs exhibit perfect or near-perfect correlation indicating deterministic redundancy likely induced by preprocessing or feature engineering.
Verified pairs such as Fwd PSH Flags & SYN Flag Count, Fwd Header Length & Fwd Header Length.1, and Packet Length Mean & Average Packet Size confirm strong redundancy signals.
No evidence supports these correlations arising naturally from network traffic properties within the bounded local context.
limitations: Analysis limited to bounded local dependency cluster; global dataset-wide duplication checks were not performed.

findings: Multiple feature pairs within the strongly correlated cluster exhibit near-perfect redundancy.
Representative features Average Packet Size, Packet Length Mean, and Fwd Packet Length Mean show moderate shortcut-like predictive signals individually.
These findings indicate that redundant feature pairs contribute to shortcut-like signals in the dataset.
limitations: Shortcut verification was performed on a subset of representative features rather than exhaustively on all redundant pairs.

findings: Multiple feature pairs in the cluster show perfect or near-perfect correlation, confirming strong redundancy.
'Fwd Header Length' is low-cardinality and near-constant, reducing effective feature dimensionality.
Local label-topology consistency checks reveal conflicts, indicating unstable dependencies around redundant features.
Dependency profiles are not narrowly concentrated, suggesting redundancy impacts dependency stability broadly.
contradictions: High label neighborhood conflicts and duplicate label conflicts around 'Fwd Header Length' indicate local contradictions in dependency stability.
limitations: Analysis limited to features within the provided local dependency cluster; broader dataset effects not assessed.

findings: Multiple feature pairs in the cluster exhibit perfect or near-perfect correlation, confirming strong redundancy.
Fwd PSH Flags and SYN Flag Count show perfect correlation and low entropy with dominant values, indicating low variability and redundancy.
Removing or consolidating these redundant features is likely to alter class-conditioned patterns and distributional properties due to their deterministic relationships.
limitations: Analysis limited to local cluster and specific feature pairs; global dataset effects not assessed.
